integration_outcome.build_pass_correction: Corrected 2026-07-20. The false value was the HARNESS-NOT-BUILD bug (fixed 34f253c on 07-17, AFTER this run on 07-13): all four SmokeUnavailable/HarnessError paths hardcoded VerifyResult(False, False), discarding Gate 1's real verdict. This run hit that path because all three clients deferred Gate 3 with no authored smoke flows. Ground truth from phaseB_verify.log: android '==> Android build OK', ios '** BUILD SUCCEEDED **', web '✓ built in 1.32s' — zero failure markers in any of the three.

blockers.issue: Web CometChat chat/calling UI is MISSING from the committed feature-branch source. The running docker web build serves a full working chat (CometChat conversations, 1:1 message header with voice/video call buttons, message list/composer — .cometchat-* DOM), but git 'diff main..feature/cometchat-integration -- web' shows ONLY package.json/lock, api/endpoints.ts, api/types.ts, cometchat/errors.ts, cometchat/ids.ts changed — NO chat component, NO @cometchat/chat-uikit-react import, NO /messages route, NO CometChatMessages/MessageHeader anywhere in web/src (34 tsx/ts files, all grep-clean). So the deliverable web app was built from an uncommitted/lost version; rebuilding from HEAD yields the SDK deps + token endpoint but NO chat. Likely lost when the Phase B re-integration hit the usage limit and resumed with --components android,ios (per HANDOFF), skipping a web re-commit. Additionally, the running build's dispute-GROUP conversation header has NO call buttons (group voice/video calling absent on web) though 1:1 does.

CometChat UIKit React v6 renders every component functional-but-UNSTYLED (no message bubbles, avatars/icons/call-buttons collapse to blank boxes, composer looks broken) when the app does not import the kit's design-token stylesheet '@cometchat/chat-uikit-react/css-variables.css'. The chat is fully operable (messages send/receive, calls place) so the failure is purely visual and silent.

Calls between clients fail immediately as 'Call Busy' / disconnect the instant they are initiated when EITHER side has a STALE/ghost active call in the SDK (a call surface that did not tear down, an app killed mid-call, or a lingering session). CometChat reports that user BUSY across ALL their sessions, so every new incoming call auto-rejects as 'Call Busy'. Neither the web nor the mobile UIKit clears a stale active call on (re)login, so a user can get permanently stuck 'busy' until the SDK state is reset. Aggravated by the same user being logged into multiple clients — any one busy/ghost session makes the user busy everywhere.

W6-ROOT — web calls can never connect under Vite: @cometchat/chat-uikit-react v6.5.3 dist/index.js resolves the calls SDK via a bare CommonJS require() inside its ESM bundle (try{mb=require('@cometchat/calls-sdk-javascript')}catch{} → pb=mb?.CometChatCalls). In a Vite/browser ESM build require is undefined, the throw is swallowed, pb stays null, and CometChatUIKit.enableCalling() SILENTLY no-ops — CometChatCalls.init() never runs, no RTCPeerConnection is ever constructed (Gate 6 CC_WEBRTC_STATS: {ice_events:[],pcs:[]}), answered calls drop to 'Missed Call'. This is the layer BELOW W5: even with CometChatUIKit.init() correctly wired, calling is dead on any Vite app without build.commonjsOptions.transformMixedEsModules.

G6-HOLLOW — every single-client Gate 6 choreography step was a silent no-op for the project's entire history: YAML 1.1 parses the bare key 'on:' as boolean True, so step.get('on') returned None, targets resolved empty, and do/await steps 'passed' without executing (steps_run still counted them). No harness run ever actually PLACED or ANSWERED a call — only both: steps ran. Prior Gate 6 verdicts (including the W6 'signals+answers but never connects' framing and the 'web<->web fails identically so it is environmental' control-run conclusion) were built on hollow steps plus human manual repro. Fixed 2026-07-15: load_choreography normalizes the on: key; the orchestrator now RAISES on a target-less do/await step (anti-hollow guard); self_test fixture added. All pre-fix Gate 6 result.json files should be treated as connect-evidence-free.

W11 — web calls wedge the buyer/seller pair BUSY after any abnormal call end: the integration registers NO CometChat.addCallListener (onIncomingCallCancelled / onCallEndedMessageReceived) and never calls CometChat.endCall(sessionId) — grep of web/src returns 0 hits. A client that disappears mid-call (tab close, crash, killed test browser) leaves the session ongoing server-side; every subsequent call to either party is instantly auto-rejected 'Call Busy' (caller) / 'Missed Call' (callee) until the zombie expires minutes later. connection.ts's clearActiveCall-on-login is a double no-op (null after fresh login; local-only anyway). Web analog of catalog I4 (iOS) and A4 (Android) ghost calls. Diagnosed AUTONOMOUSLY by the Gate 6 debug agent (verdict diagnosed, confidence high, evidence: webweb_CLEAN connected vs webweb_HARD insta-busy on identical code + its own expiry repro).

G6-ISOLATION — Gate 6 cross-run contamination: orchestrator._teardown() closed browsers WITHOUT ending an active call (and headless browser close skips beforeunload, so app-side guards cannot fire). Any run that connected a call and tore down mid-call left a zombie CometChat session server-side; the buyer/seller pair then auto-rejected every subsequent call 'Call Busy', failing the NEXT run at place_voice_call. Diagnosed AUTONOMOUSLY by the Gate 6 debug agent (verdict harness_bug, confidence high — correctly routed AWAY from the app ledger per MV-3, its clean-state re-run passed green proving no app regression). Fixed: teardown now hang_up-s every executor before stop.

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: The integration agent must COMMIT the web chat UI it builds (the CometChat React components + /messages route). Verify Phase B completeness by rebuilding each component from committed source and re-running Gate 3 — a runtime green against a stale docker build hides an uncommitted integration. Also wire GROUP call buttons on the web message header (group voice/video), not just 1:1.

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: Re-integrate + COMMIT the web CometChat chat/calling UI (see W5) and ensure the web UIKit settings are initialized for calling (the 'uiKitSettings not available' console error must be gone). Then re-run Gate 6 (gate6/run.py one_to_one_voice) to confirm android<->web 1:1 voice actually connects. If it still fails with a committed, correctly-configured web build, escalate to a CometChat calls-SDK / cross-platform WebRTC interop investigation.
improvement_suggestions.motivated_by: Gate 6 (cross-platform interop): a 1:1 VOICE call android-buyer -> web-seller does NOT connect. Signaling works — the caller's outgoing surface mounts, the web callee receives the incoming-call popup (.cometchat-incoming-call with __button-accept/__button-decline) and answering it (the REAL accept button, verified both via the Gate 6 harness AND a manual click) — but the media session never establishes: no ongoing-call UI mounts on either side, the android caller returns to chat, and the web call is logged as a 'Missed Call'. Reproduced 2x via gate6/run.py (choreography marketplace-1to1-voice fails at step 4 'call_connected', 30s timeout on BOTH clients) + once manually. The web client repeatedly logs the CometChat console error 'uiKitSettings not available' during call init — a concrete signal the web calling UIKit settings are not fully initialized (consistent with W5: the web CometChat chat/calling UI is missing from committed source; the running docker build is from an uncommitted/lost version). CAVEAT: a headless-Chromium <-> android-emulator WebRTC media path over docker/loopback could also contribute; the uiKitSettings signal + W5 point at incomplete web integration as the primary suspect.

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: The web UIKit integration skill/quickstart MUST state that '@cometchat/chat-uikit-react/css-variables.css' has to be imported once app-wide (before local overrides); without it the entire kit UI is unstyled though functional. Add it to the minimal 'render your first component' snippet, not just a theming appendix.
improvement_suggestions.motivated_by: CometChat UIKit React v6 renders every component functional-but-UNSTYLED (no message bubbles, avatars/icons/call-buttons collapse to blank boxes, composer looks broken) when the app does not import the kit's design-token stylesheet '@cometchat/chat-uikit-react/css-variables.css'. The chat is fully operable (messages send/receive, calls place) so the failure is purely visual and silent.

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: The calling skill/quickstart should show clearing a stale active call on connect (CometChat.clearActiveCall() after login) and warn that getActiveCall() persisting from a prior/ungracefully-ended session makes the user permanently BUSY (every call auto-rejects 'Call Busy'). Also document multi-session busy semantics (a user is busy if ANY session has an active call) and that call-end/reject events must be reconciled against getActiveCall().sessionID before tearing down UI.

Aggravated by the same user being logged into multiple clients — any one busy/ghost session makes the user busy everywhere.

retracted_blockers.issue: Gate 6 (cross-platform interop): a 1:1 VOICE call android-buyer -> web-seller does NOT connect. Signaling works — the caller's outgoing surface mounts, the web callee receives the incoming-call popup (.cometchat-incoming-call with __button-accept/__button-decline) and answering it (the REAL accept button, verified both via the Gate 6 harness AND a manual click) — but the media session never establishes: no ongoing-call UI mounts on either side, the android caller returns to chat, and the web call is logged as a 'Missed Call'. Reproduced 2x via gate6/run.py (choreography marketplace-1to1-voice fails at step 4 'call_connected', 30s timeout on BOTH clients) + once manually. The web client repeatedly logs the CometChat console error 'uiKitSettings not available' during call init — a concrete signal the web calling UIKit settings are not fully initialized (consistent with W5: the web CometChat chat/calling UI is missing from committed source; the running docker build is from an uncommitted/lost version). CAVEAT: a headless-Chromium <-> android-emulator WebRTC media path over docker/loopback could also contribute; the uiKitSettings signal + W5 point at incomplete web integration as the primary suspect.
retracted_blockers.retraction_reason: MISDIAGNOSIS — one failure, two competing causes; the other one is proven. W6 blames W5 (web chat/calling UI missing from committed source). W5 was FIXED on 2026-07-14 by 00efab6, and the same choreography still failed afterwards: gate6/one_to_one_voice_w6verify/result.json (07-15 16:39) and …_w6verify2 (16:46) both record step 4 call_connected 'timed out (20000ms)' on BOTH clients. So the named cause was falsified by fixing it. The operative cause is W6-ROOT (tagged SDK), verified verbatim in the shipped bundle: @cometchat/chat-uikit-react v6.5.3 dist/index.js contains `var mb=null;try{mb=require("@cometchat/calls-sdk-javascript")}catch(e){}var pb=mb?.CometChatCalls;` — under Vite ESM `require` is undefined, pb stays null, enableCalling() no-ops and no RTCPeerConnection is built. Additionally W6's own evidence is disavowed in the same report: G6-HOLLOW names W6 explicitly and says all pre-fix Gate 6 verdicts were built on hollow no-op steps and must be treated as connect-evidence-free. Tag compounded it: W6 is `agent` (blames the integrator) when the defect is SDK packaging.
